site_description: The playful encyclopedia of cognitive biases
about_intro: Cognipedia is a playful encyclopedia of cognitive biases. Discover how your brain tricks you through interactive situations and quizzes.
about_codex: Cognitive biases are organized into 4 families, inspired by the Cognitive Bias Codex:
about_codex_too_much_information: Too much information — our brain filters and only retains part of the available information.
about_codex_not_enough_meaning: Not enough meaning — we fill in the gaps by inventing stories and patterns.
about_codex_need_to_act_fast: Need to act fast — to avoid missing opportunities, we take shortcuts.
about_codex_what_to_remember: What to remember? — our memory reinforces some memories and alters others.
error_404_messages_0: Blind spot bias — you were convinced this page existed.
error_404_messages_1: Dunning-Kruger effect — this URL was confident, but wrong.
error_404_messages_2: Confirmation bias — you wanted to believe, but this page doesn't exist.
error_404_messages_3: Frequency illusion — no, you haven't seen this page before.
error_404_messages_4: Halo effect — that link looked trustworthy, yet it leads nowhere.
recovery_modal_description: An account has been created to save your scores. Your recovery code lets you access your account on another device:
recovery_modal_profile_hint: You can find this code anytime on your
recovery_form_error_not_found: No account found for this code
profile_not_registered_hint: Complete your first bias to create your profile.
profile_recovery_hint: This code lets you access your account on another device or browser. Go to Cognipedia on your new device and click "Already have an account?" at the bottom of the page.
